title: Latency, Concurrency, Rate and Spend Limits, Context Cache, and Load · Contract Workshop
summary: A public-interest product team incrementally builds budgeted agent capacity and overload controller from original fixed provider, interaction, tool, approval, evaluation, and failure fixtures while retaining prior Python, HTTP, prompt, loop, security, accessibility, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of budgeted agent capacity and overload controller while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit th…
objectives: Measure queue, provider time to first event, generation, tool, approval, retry, serialization, and end-to-end latency by outcome and percentile.
Bound sessions, provider calls, tool calls, per-tenant work, queues, task groups, connection pools, and downstream effects with fair admission.
Enforce project and tenant requests, tokens, daily and rolling spend, retry, model, and tool budgets before work starts and while it runs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Representative Load Transfer
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: agentpy-representative-load-transfer-1tzhsn" that uses case 5343 to bound queueing, concurrency, tokens, spend, latency, rate limits, context caching, overload rejection, and representative load evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not contact provider APIs, invoke Gemini models, import or claim native google-genai, google-adk, or Pydantic behavior, open sockets, read or write host files, use credentials, execute generated code, launch browsers, reach databases or telemetry backends, or claim live privacy, load, safety, cost, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, google-genai 2.11.0, google-adk 2.4.0, Pydantic 2.13.4, fixed provider fixtures, fake transports, controlled local services, disposable cloud projects, representative eval sets, accessibility, privacy and security review, load, canary, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Representative Load Transfer.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Representative Load Transfer into observable performance evidence. For Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Representative Load Transfer, keep Representative Load Transfer connected to executable Python model, interaction, tool, policy, state, approval, evaluation, security, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-representative-load-transfer-1tzhsn
# Competency: agentpy-representative-load-transfer.
# Case 5343: a clean package, canary, and recovery drill.
# Operating constraint: sensitive actions require a trustworthy user approval bound to exact arguments.
# Changed case: inject cancellation, retry, repeated-call, checkpoint, or replay failure and recover without duplicate effects.
def evidence_agentpy_representative_load_transfer_1tzhsn(requests: int = 120, completed_requests: int = 114, rejected_overload: int = 6, cache_hits: int = 32, budget_breaches: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1tzhsn = "1tzhsn-nshzt1-5343"
    assert evidence_variant_1tzhsn
    evidence_axes_1tzhsn = (
        "authority1tzhsn",
        "interaction5343",
        "toolnshzt1",
        "state1tzhsn5343",
        "evaluation53431tzhsn",
        "recoverynshzt15343",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1tzhsn)) == 6
    if requests < 20 or completed_requests < 0 or rejected_overload < 0:
        return False, requests, "load-count"
    if completed_requests + rejected_overload != requests:
        return False, completed_requests, "load-reconciliation"
    if cache_hits < 0 or cache_hits > completed_requests:
        return False, cache_hits, "cache-accounting"
    if budget_breaches > 0:
        return False, budget_breaches, "resource-budget-breach"
    assert completed_requests + rejected_overload == requests and budget_breaches == 0
    return True, completed_requests, "bounded-agent-capacity"
